_note: Starter dataset. Keyed by lowercased country name. Period-agnostic for now; later we key facts by year range.

france.name: France
france.note: One of the oldest unified states in Europe. Fought England for control of mainland territory across the medieval and early-modern periods.

england.name: England
england.leader: Monarch Charles III (UK); Prime Minister leads government
england.note: An island kingdom that long sought and held continental footholds in France before consolidating as part of the United Kingdom.

united kingdom.name: United Kingdom
united kingdom.leader: Monarch Charles III; PM leads government
united kingdom.note: Union of England, Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland; former center of the largest empire in history.

germany.name: Germany
germany.note: Unified in 1871; its borders shifted dramatically across both World Wars.

russia.name: Russia
russia.note: Largest country on Earth by area; successor state to the Russian Empire and Soviet Union.

united states.name: United States
united states.note: Expanded across North America through the 19th century via purchase, treaty and annexation.
